data.title: AerCap Holdings is the top performing trading companies and distributors stock YTD
data.description:
The S&P 500 industrials sector has delivered a robust performance in 2025, with a year to date performance of up +18.2%.

The sector ranked as the third-best-performing sector year to date and, outpacing the broader benchmark S&P 500 (SP500).

As we look at the sub-sectors within the industrials sector, below is a list of the top 10 trading companies and distributors stocks ranked by their YTD performance. The list also includes Quant Ratings for each company.

The list is topped by AerCap Holdings N.V. (AER), with a YTD performance of +51.75% and a Strong Buy Quant Rating. Xometry (XMTR) follows with +48.36% YTD performance, and WESCO International (WCC) rounds out the top three at +39.58% YTD performance with a Buy rating. QXO (QXO) and Air Lease (AL) complete the top five performers in the sector.

Here is the list:

  1. AerCap Holdings N.V. (AER), YTD perf: +51.75%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  2. Xometry (XMTR), YTD perf: +48.36%, Quant Rating: Hold

data.title: Retail shocker: Generation Z and Millennials power Barnes & Noble's bookstore turnaround
data.description:
Barnes & Noble's retail comeback is one of the most surprising turnarounds in the retail sector, as the chain defied predictions that physical bookstores were destined for extinction.

Just 25 years ago, the company seemed headed for the same fate as Borders, Book World, BookPeople, WaldenBooks, and B. Dalton after closing locations due to the rise of Amazon (AMZN) and e-commerce book buying. While Barnes & Noble survived, for years the company cycled through CEOs and struggled to find its identity.

The turning point came in 2019 when Elliott Advisors acquired Barnes & Noble and brought in James Daunt. The chain reverted to its bookstore roots, and Daunt gave individual store managers unprecedented autonomy to curate their own inventory based on local tastes. Barnes & Noble also became a more inviting space to relax or hold a small meeting during the same time period that certain Starbucks (SBUX) and Panera (PANERA) started to resemble a mosh pit of pick-up orders. Barnes & Noble has also benefited from the BookTok phenomenon on TikTok (TIKTOK), as the social media movement made book buying feel participatory and social rather than solitary.

After bottoming out around 600 stores, Barnes & Noble opened approximately 30 new stores in 2023, 61 in 2024, and 67 in 2025. As of November 25, there were 721 Barnes & Noble locations. The company plans to open 60 additional stores in 2026.

 

 

 

The Barnes & Noble revival is also seen by some analysts as representing a broader cultural shift. Physical books have proven surprisingly resilient against digital alternatives, with print book sales consistently outperforming e-books in recent years. Younger generations, despite being digital natives, have shown strong interest in physical books, vinyl records, and other tangible media. Recent surveys indicate that a large percentage of Generation Z (currently aged 13 to 28) prefer to read print books, while Millennials (currently aged 29 to 44) have displayed a surge of nostalgia for chains such as Barnes & Noble and GameStop (GME). The potential takeaway for investors is that Barnes & Noble's story demonstrates that even in the digital age, physical retail can thrive when it offers genuine value.

Barnes & Noble is widely expected to come back to the public markets in 2026 through a combined IPO with UK bookstore chain Waterstones.

data.title: Beauty spending shows pockets of strength as wellness categories decline -- Canaccord Genuity
data.description:
Spending on health and beauty merchandise has been mixed so far this holiday season with trends indicating consumers are waiting for promotional events, and challenges continuing for health and personal care.

For the two-week period beginning Cyber Monday and concluding December 14, Canaccord Genuity Capital Markets found nearly all categories within the beauty segment seeing increased sales for the period, while wellness items -- including analgesics, gastro and upper respiratory treatments -- down as much as 8.5% from a year ago. Canaccord’s Susan Anderson attributes this to a late start to the cough, cold and flu season.

Beauty demand was positive but slowed from the same period last year. The strongest performing category was skin care, up 5.6% versus +7.7% in 2024, followed by a 1.3% year-over-year gain in hair care but down from 2.8% growth last year. Fragrance saw the most dramatic shift. While sales were up 1.3% this year, this was a significant change from +8.6% during the 2024 holiday season with the deceleration largely attributed to sluggish sales for prestige fragrances as consumers trade-down to more affordable mass market options.

Looking at individual names within each category, e.l.f. Beauty’s (ELF) major brands tracked by Canaccord experienced growth for the 2-week period. While e.l.f. (ELF) sales continue to normalize, skin care brand Naturium realized another double-digit growth rate of +74.7% year-over-year, accelerating from +69.6% sales growth last year.

Kenvue’s (KVUE) Tylenol and Neutrogena sales were down during the two-week period but improved from a year ago.

For Coty (COTY), the mass cosmetics category has been weaker globally compared to prestige, especially in the U.S., with legacy brands like CoverGirl harder hit. CoverGirl sales were down 8.8% during this period, worsening from -8.0% last year, while Sally Hansen sales fell 8.9% versus -7.1% in the same period last year.

Canaccord’s Anderson saw mostly negative results for Helen of Troy (HELE), with Health, Wellness & Beauty all seeing sales decelerate from a year ago, and Home & Outdoor brands also generally weaker. Water filtration brand PUR saw sales slide 7.1% year-over-year from -5.9% last year despite units being up 12.9%.

Finally, demand among Interparfums’ (IPAR) brands was also mixed with Coach and Guess perfume sales down, but Jimmy Choo and Donna Karan up versus -9.7% and +28.1% last year.

data.title: Deere edges up after a six-day losing streak
data.description:
Shares of Deere & Company (DE) rose 0.19% to $467.09 in the afternoon trade on Wednesday, snapping a six-session losing streak.

The stock had fallen around 4% between December 16 and December 23, while the S&P 500,  rose 1.61% over the same period.

The equipment maker has been under pressure amid broader concerns over equipment costs and regulatory pressures. Investors have been digesting the impact of a $12 billion U.S. aid package for farmers announced earlier this month, coupled with President Trump’s call for lower farm equipment prices and the removal of environmental restrictions affecting machinery. Analysts say Deere remains exposed to fluctuations in crop demand and input costs, even as government support aims to ease pressures on farmers.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating system, DE has a score of 2.76 out of 5, with a A+ in profitability, but offset by weaker grades of C- in momentum, D in valuation and a D+ both in terms of growth and revisions.

An analyst said Deere & Company remains a leading company in agricultural machinery, despite recent headwinds, noting, “The stock has experienced top- and bottom-line headwinds, but I argue it remains well-positioned to benefit from future tailwinds,” adding that the company’s premium margins and strong market position support a bullish outlook.

Turning to the Wall Street, analysts are bullish, with 14 out of 25 analysts rate the stock with a buy or higher, while 11 suggest to hold.

Shares have fallen about 5% over the past month but are up 10.07% year to date.

data.title: Nipsco ordered to keep coal power plant in Indiana running
data.description:
Northern Indiana Public Service Company said Wednesday it was instructed by federal authorities to keep its R.M. Schahfer power plant operating past its scheduled shutdown at the end of 2025.

Under the order, the Indiana-based coal facility must continue running for at least 90 days starting from the date the directive was issued, the utility said.

The mandate reflects a broader trend across the U.S. power sector, where utilities are postponing coal plant retirements amid rising electricity demand. Much of that demand is being driven by rapid expansion of data centers, while higher natural gas prices have made coal generation more economically attractive in some regions.

The move also aligns with policy signals from the Trump administration, which has pushed to revive domestic coal production. President Donald Trump signed executive orders in April aimed at encouraging greater use of coal-fired power.

Nipsco, which is owned by NiSource (NI), had previously announced plans to retire the plant’s remaining two coal-fired units by the close of 2025.

data.title: Tesla faces NHTSA investigation into Model 3 emergency door releases
data.description:
The NHTSA confirmed on Wednesday that it will conduct an investigation into complaints about the mechanical door releases of 2022 Tesla (TSLA) Model 3 vehicles. The investigation will cover 179,071 vehicles. 

The Office of Defects Investigation received a petition on November 24 requesting the defect investigation into the mechanical door release. The petition cited allegations that the mechanical door release is hidden, unlabeled, and not intuitive to locate during an emergency.

The NHTSA received one complaint of an injury tied to the issue.

"During a head-on collision on [XXX] in Georgia, the electrical system in my 2022 Tesla Model 3 lost power and the electric door releases stopped functioning. The vehicle interior caught fire and I was trapped inside. I was unaware of the location of the hidden mechanical emergency door release because it is not visibly labeled, not explained upon delivery, and not intuitive in an emergency. I was forced to climb to the back seat and break the rear passenger window with my legs to escape while the interior was burning. I suffered a broken hip, broken arm, and later required a full hip replacement. The mechanical emergency door release was concealed and unlabeled, resulting in entrapment during a fire, which is a violation of FMVSS 206 and presents a continuing and life-threatening safety hazard to all drivers and passengers."

Separately, there are media reports indicating occupants of a Tesla Model 3 or emergency responders struggled to open the vehicle doors.

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) dipped 1.0% in Wednesday morning trading.

data.title: S&P 500 hits record high on Christmas Eve as Wall Street rises
data.description:
Wall Street’s major averages inched higher on Wednesday during the shortened Christmas Eve trading session.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.1%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.2%, and the blue-chip Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) was +0.5%. 

Despite a relatively subdued trading session, the S&P 500 climbed to an intraday record high of 6,928.97. The move followed Tuesday’s milestone close—the index’s 38th record finish of the year—highlighting the market’s continued resilience even after a turbulent 2025.

From a sector vantage point, nine of the S&P 500’s 11 sectors were in the green with consumer staples at the top of the list. Going the other way, communication services was the weakest performer so far. 

U.S. Treasury yields were slightly lower. The shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved down 1 basis point to 3.53%, while the U.S.10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) dipped 1 basis point to 4.15%. 

On the economic front, traders received some labor market data in the form of an unexpectedly lower initial jobless claims report. 

As for stocks that were on the move, shares of Nike (NKE) were +4.8% while Intel (INTC) were -1.2%.

For reference, markets will close at 13:00 ET today and will be closed on Thursday.

data.title: Business uncertainty over revenue growth rises in December: Atlanta Fed
data.description:
Business uncertainty about revenue growth (smoothed) for the four quarters ahead increased to 3.83% in December from 3.80% in the prior month, according to an Atlanta Fed survey released on Wednesday.

Also for the year ahead, smoothed employment growth uncertainty dipped to 4.32% from 4.37% in November.

data.title: AST SpaceMobile launches BlueBird 6; shares fall amid overvaluation concerns
data.description:
AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) said Wednesday it has successfully launched the BlueBird 6 satellite, in what it calls "the largest commercial communications array ever deployed in low earth orbit."

BlueBird 6 is capable of delivering high-speed 4G and 5G directly to standard, unmodified smartphones and government devices from space, the company said. 

The satellite, which is thrice the size and has 10 times the capacity of its six satellites currently in orbit, launched from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre in Sriharikota, India, on a Launch Vehicle Mark-3 rocket.

"The satellite is designed to enable peak data rates of up to 120 Mbps directly to standard, unmodified mobile devices, supporting voice, full data, and video applications from space," AST SpaceMobile said.

The satellite was assembled, integrated, and tested at the company's manufacturing facilities in Midland, Texas. The company said it continues to be on track to launch 45–60 satellites by the end of 2026, with launches planned every one or two months on average.

ASTS shares rose as much as 9.4% to $92.90 but turned negative and are currently down nearly 4.1% at $82.17 in early open market trading on the NASDAQ.

Over 13.7M shares had already changed hands by 10:42 am ET, exceeding its three-month average trading volume of 13.6M.

Concerns about the stock's overvaluation have recently been expressed by analysts on Wall Street and Seeking Alpha. ASTS has risen 336% so far this year, while the benchmark S&P index rose 17.5% for the same duration.

Earlier in December, Seeking Alpha analyst Stone Fox Capital said, "The stock is far too expensive for realistic financial targets given repeated launch delays and uncertainty around execution, despite potential for speculative price spikes."

Seeking Alpha Quant and SA analysts on average have rated the stock a "hold," while Wall Street analysts rated it a "buy."
